free_text: Elastic and inelastic scattering of neutrons in the
energy range 1.0 to 5.0 MeV by natural niobium
AWRE 6 MV Van de Graaff accelerator.
T(p,n)He-3 reaction, tritium gas target with
a 0.0003 cm thick Mo foil window.
Energy spreads of incident neutron varied
from 50 keV at 1.0 MeV incident n-energy, to 30 keV
at 3.0 MeV, to 20 keV at 5.0 MeV.
Hollow right cylinder 5.01 cm long, 2.00 cm outer
diam.,1.20 cm inner diam. and purity better than 99%.
Time of flight, flight path = 1.747 m.
* The well known neutron group energies from
n-scattering of iron were used as time calibration
and a check on incident neutron energy.
Liquid scintillator
The peak due to the 29 keV level was impossible to
separate from that due to elastic events and in all
cases it is included in elastic X-sect.
Neutron flux attenuation and multiple scattering
corrections were applied by using the MAGGIE code
and the evaluated file DFN 79 of the UKNDL.
The scattering at 45 deg from
the polythene sample was measured.
- The neutron
detection system and experimental procedures described
- methods
of cross sections calculation, corrections for multiple
scattering, flux attenuation and finite angular
resolution of the source-scatterer geometry and
error estimation.
- MAGGIE code
Errors were estimated in the way described
in AWRE-O-89/70; one standard deviation in every case.
The work was originated to answer a request for
improved data for use in fast reactor design.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Part of information from MONITOR moved to METHOD.
MONIT-REF, REL-REF were added. PART-DET deleted.
Differential cross sections were measured at
ten angles from 30. to 135.degr., at 0.5 MeV intervals
between 1.5 and 5.0 MeV incident neutron energy.
